build.observation: xcodebuild -scheme Kite -configuration Debug build: ** BUILD SUCCEEDED ** with zero warnings (confirmed after fresh xcodegen).

test.observation: xcodebuild test -scheme Kite -destination 'platform=macOS' -only-testing:KiteTests: 123 Swift Testing cases in 19 suites + 19 XCTest snapshot cases in 4 suites = 142 tests green in 40.2s. New this milestone: GitQueue (3), RepoFocus (4), RepoStore (4), RepoSidebarModel ext (1), BranchListModel (6), StatusHeaderModel (7), BranchListSnapshot (5), StatusHeaderSnapshot (5). 107 prior tests still green. KiteUITests NOT run (TCC blocker per explicit direction).

swiftformat.observation: swiftformat Sources Tests --lint: 0/76 files require formatting.

snapshotMd5Audit.observation: All 10 new reference PNGs md5-distinct. BranchListSnapshotTests: 603206ef (dark), 4f580a76 (detachedHead), 13ec1365 (emptyState), 2d520d49 (localOnly), bd21787e (withRemotes-light). StatusHeaderSnapshotTests: a11af65a (clean), e5adb52c (dark), 0403a343 (detached), dfc8a4c8 (stagedModified-light), fe2637b0 (withAheadBehind). Dark vs light of same composition differ by ≥2M-bit MD5 distance — the `NSHostingController.view.appearance = .darkAqua` fix from AGENTS.md Established patterns is correctly applied.

nonBlockingIssues.description: CompletionGate is `@unchecked Sendable` with no justification doc-comment — same pattern we flagged on FSWatcher in foundation. Add one-line justification: NSLock serializes reads/writes of fired+waiter; CheckedContinuation is single-writer/single-reader.

`rootTask: Task<Void, Never>?` is declared and cancelled on shutdown/deinit but never assigned. Reserved for M5 sidecar streams per handoff. Either delete until needed, or add a `// M5-reserved` comment so it reads as infrastructure rather than dead code.

`select(_:)` writes lastOpenedRepo to persistence AND forwards to `repoStore?.focus(on:)` which ALSO writes the same key. Handoff justifies the double-write for nil-repoStore configurations; add an inline comment so the next reader doesn't think it's forgotten duplication.

`probeDetachedHead` fires three sequential `focus.queue.run` calls — each a subprocess spawn. ~15-30ms overhead per reload beyond the branch listings. Consider collapsing into one or two commands. Tighten before M5 auto-fetch tightens timing.
`lastFSTick` is assigned but never read — pure dead field. Same disposition for StatusHeaderModel.lastFSTick (L28): delete until needed, or add intent comment.

`.onChange(of: focus.lastChangeAt) { Task { await model.reload(for: focus) } }` spawns unstructured Task per tick — no cancellation of prior in-flight reload. Same pattern in StatusHeaderView L33. Fast FSEvents burst can queue 4-6 git probes. Non-blocking (GitQueue serializes, correctness preserved) but wasteful; consolidate into a RepoFocus-level 'reloadAll' or a per-model serial-Task handle before M5.

The `pills` ViewBuilder with nested conditionals is readable but not structurally unit-testable. Extracting the branch → [PillSpec] mapping into a private func would let us add a 'gone + ahead present → gone only' regression test without a snapshot diff.

`StatusHeaderContent.placeholder` duplicates the main body's HStack chrome (icon + text + frame/padding/background). DRY violation: chrome change requires two updates. Extract to a shared `headerChrome` modifier.

Under tight split widths (<300px) the `Label(count, systemImage: arrow.up)` ahead/behind cluster may render with iconography suppressed — verify legibility via a new testNarrowColumn snapshot before M4 tightens the split.
Test case `testUntracked` uses XCTest-style `test` prefix while other @Test cases use descriptive names. Rename to `untracked` for consistency.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Add under Established patterns: 'Stateful view + pure content view split. SwiftUI views that own an @Observable model should split into (a) an outer `ContainerView` that pulls environment + @State + drives task/onChange lifecycle, and (b) an inner `ContentView` whose init takes plain-data parameters (the model's exposed fields, not the model itself). Snapshot tests drive the inner `ContentView` directly for hermetic captures across every visual branch; XCUITests drive the `ContainerView` for the real wiring. M3-status-header's StatusHeaderView/StatusHeaderContent split is the canonical example.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: M3-status-header implemented the pattern perfectly; M3-branch-list used a less-split approach (BranchListView is monolithic + BranchRow). M4 graph container, M5 toast host, M6 branch sheet, M7 diff pane will all benefit from codification.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Add under Known gotchas → Swift actors: 'Swift actors serialize mailbox arrival but are REENTRANT across `await` suspension points inside a method body. `actor Q { func run(body) { try await body() } }` does NOT serialize two callers whose bodies suspend — caller 2's body can begin while caller 1's body is awaiting. For ops that must not overlap across suspension (e.g. subprocess that locks `.git/index.lock`), use an explicit completion-gate chain: each `run` captures the prior gate, installs its own, awaits the prior, runs body, and fires its own gate in a defer. See `Sources/Repo/GitQueue.swift` for the canonical implementation.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: GitQueue discovered this non-trivially (the feature brief's suggested trivial impl would NOT have met VAL-NET-009). Codify so M5-auto-fetch and any future per-repo actor don't re-learn it under time pressure.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Add under Testing guidance: 'Avoid `@unchecked Sendable` without a one-line doc-comment explaining why the concurrent access pattern is safe. Two instances in the codebase (FSWatcher, GitQueue.CompletionGate) are correct but provide no written justification; future maintainers have no quick way to verify safety. Minimum bar: `// Safe: <what protects which fields>`.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: Repeated pattern across two features. Codify before M4 introduces more actor-adjacent types.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Add under §4 Shared state rules: 'Per-focus observation fan-out. Every @Observable view model that observes `focus.lastChangeAt` spawns its own reload per FSWatcher tick — BranchListModel, StatusHeaderModel. As M4/M5/M7 add LogModel/GraphModel/DiffModel, N concurrent git probes per tick becomes wasteful. Workers introducing a 3rd+ focus-observing model should either (a) consolidate into a shared RepoDetailModel that fans out, or (b) open a scrutiny ticket to refactor before committing. GitQueue serialization keeps correctness but not cost.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: Two observers today is fine; four observers tomorrow is a reload storm. Documented boundary before it becomes a retrofit.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Document the 'probe with minimum subprocess count' discipline: when a model needs multiple git queries to compute state, prefer composing fewer commands (`git rev-parse A B C` over three separate invocations). Each `Git.run` spawns /usr/bin/git and waits for exit; three probes = ~30ms of pure process overhead. BranchListModel.probeDetachedHead currently fires three sequential probes; a single `rev-parse --symbolic-full-name` + `rev-parse --short HEAD` composition would halve it.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: M4 graph and M7 diff will both be tempted to do per-field probing. Codify the 'minimize subprocesses' principle now.

anyOrphansOrFailures.note: Not scoped to M3 — scheduled for M5-fetch ('After successful fetch, branch list ahead/behind updates without manual ⌘R'). M3 wires the refresh mechanism (BranchListView reloads on focus.lastChangeAt); M5 must trigger it after fetch completion. Correctly scoped in features.json.

The full FSEvents → focus.lastChangeAt → model.reload loop is now closed end-to-end by StatusHeaderView's wiring. The authored XCUITest `testStatusHeaderRefreshesOnExternalChange` will exercise the 2s gate on a TCC-accepting host. M1-fs-watcher originally claimed this as 'partial' pending wiring; M3-status-header's wiring closes it.

GitQueue.serializesConcurrentOps directly demonstrates mail-ordered body execution across suspension — even stronger than the minimum VAL-NET-009 wording ('two simultaneous ops run sequentially'). The CompletionGate chain is the correct implementation, verified.

Window-close cancellation of in-flight git processes is covered at the mechanism level (Task.cancel → Process.terminate via M1-git-engine + RepoFocus.shutdown) but the full 'close window, pgrep -P shows zero' check is a manual step per features.json and requires an interactive run. Unit test `cancellationPropagates` confirms CancellationError propagates through the queue, so the wiring is sound.

carryOverObservations.note: Git.run 64KB pipe-buffer deadlock (L55), Git.run cancel race (L82), missing GitError.remoteRejected (L5), DiffParser quoted-path form (L216), ProgressParser consume-all (L40), LogParser Swift-5.10 if-expr (L59), RefKind.head dead case (L12), Persistence per-field keys (L27), PersistenceStore silent swallow (L160), setWindowFrame debounce (L105), FSWatcher.start() post-stop silent no-op (L88), FSWatcher @unchecked-Sendable rationale (L24), README swiftformat flag order (L38), EmptyCommands VAL-UI-009 regression (L11) — all still open, none regressed, all scoped to later milestones per the original synthesis.

SettingsRootsTab degenerate snapshots (MD5 d10d012d × 4), RepoSidebar dark-mode byte-identical (MD5 b519ca7d/47fe1945), xcodegen auto-resources missing excludes in project.yml, RepoSidebarModel.refresh double-trigger, SettingsRootsTab transient models duplication, PersistenceStore load/write still swallow errors, DiscoveredRepo.id = URL (symlink duplication) — all still open, all scoped for post-TCC-unblock user-testing or later milestones. NONE of M3's new snapshots fell into the degenerate trap (the NSAppearance fix is correctly applied).

Per-tick reload fan-out across @Observable models (BranchListModel + StatusHeaderModel → 2 reloads per tick → 6+ git probes after the detached-HEAD probe multi-step). No correctness impact; efficiency concern only.

Visually verify Dark appearance renders correctly at narrow (260px) and wide (520px) middle-column widths.
